forecast-period.text: A high pressure system will become established south of SA today, then moving eastwards over the weekend to be centred over the Tasman Sea on Monday. A low pressure system well to the south will link to a trough over the far west late Monday and into Tuesday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Possible light showers about windward coasts and ranges. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures well below average. South to southeasterly winds, fresh about the northeast and coastal districts. Winds shifting easterly in the west.

Possible light showers about windward coasts and ranges and the Lower South East. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures below average in the west, grading to well below average in the northeast. South to southeasterly winds, tending northeasterly in the west. Local afternoon coastal sea breezes.
Mostly dry. Maximum temperatures near average in the southwest, grading to below average in the northeast. Generally southeast to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Mostly dry. Maximum temperatures slightly above average about the west and southern agricultural area, gradient to below average in the northeast. Generally southeast to northeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: broadly less than 2mm south of around Coober Pedy, possibly reaching 5 to 10mm in parts of the Riverland, Mid North, Flinders and southern pastoral districts. Isolated falls of 10 to 25mm possible with thunderstorms.
